sources.name: UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA)
sources.dataset: Hepatitis C in England and the UK

sources.name: World Health Organization
sources.dataset: Global Health Sector Strategy on Viral Hepatitis

metadata.methodology: Treatment starts are counted as the number of people commencing direct-acting antiviral (DAA) therapy in a given calendar year, as reported by NHS England's Hepatitis C Elimination Programme. Undiagnosed estimates are modelled by UKHSA using prevalence surveys, treatment data, and mortality adjustments. Cure rate reflects sustained virological response at 12 weeks (SVR12) from clinical audits.
knownIssues: Treatment start figures for 2020-2021 were significantly affected by COVID-19 service disruptions
Undiagnosed estimates carry substantial uncertainty (±15%) due to reliance on modelling assumptions
Pre-2017 treatment data includes some interferon-based regimens with lower cure rates
